verdict: The AI displacement risk for Athletic Coaches is moderate, with some tasks being automated but still requiring human judgment and coaching skills.

reasoning_steps.title: Task automation
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like planning practice sessions (task 1), providing training direction and encouragement (task 2), and instructing individuals or groups in sports rules (task 4) are already being automated by AI-powered coaching tools.

reasoning_steps.title: Human judgment required
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks that require human judgment, such as adjusting coaching techniques based on athletes' strengths and weaknesses (task 3), analyzing the strengths and weaknesses of opposing teams (task 8), and counseling student athletes on academic and personal issues (task 7), are less likely to be automated.

reasoning_steps.title: Coaching skills still valuable
reasoning_steps.body: While AI can provide data-driven insights, human coaching skills like motivation, encouragement, and emotional intelligence remain essential for building strong team relationships and achieving success in sports.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Task automation and AI-powered coaching tools are already being used in the athletic coaching industry.
Human judgment and coaching skills remain essential for building strong team relationships and achieving success in sports.

adjacent_careers.name: Sports Manager
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both athletic coaches and sports managers work closely with teams to achieve success, but sports managers have more administrative responsibilities.

adjacent_careers.name: Physical Education Teacher
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Physical education teachers teach physical education classes and develop curricula, while athletic coaches focus on team training and competition.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered coaching tool launches to help athletes improve performance
recent_developments.impact: This new tool may automate some tasks, but human coaches will still be needed to provide personalized guidance and support.

recent_developments.headline: Universities update curricula to assume AI in the loop
recent_developments.impact: Programs add prompt-craft, verification, and AI-direction skills as core competencies.

quotes.quote: We are at the cusp of using AI for probably the biggest positive transformation that education has ever seen — a personal tutor for every student, on every subject.
quotes.speaker: Sal Khan
quotes.title: Founder, Khan Academy

quotes.quote: If we just use AI to grade essays faster, we have failed. The opportunity is to free teachers from low-value work so they can do what only humans can do — mentor.
quotes.speaker: Linda Darling-Hammond
quotes.title: President, Learning Policy Institute; Stanford emerita

quotes.quote: Teachers are not going to be replaced by AI. Teachers who use AI well are going to replace teachers who do not.
quotes.speaker: Ethan Mollick
quotes.title: Associate Professor, Wharton; author of Co-Intelligence
